errors.runInDesktopMode: App is not in desktop mode. Run with 'npm run tauri dev' (Tauri window), not in the browser.

advisor.title: Server Advisor
advisor.tagline: Your server setup and config assistant
advisor.placeholder: Ask about setup, configs, mods, plugins, or paste errors for help.
advisor.thinkingProcess: Reading your message and calling the AI to generate a response. This usually takes a few seconds.
advisor.terminalHint: Turn on "Include server log" to send recent build or run output — the Advisor can suggest fixes.
advisor.proRequired: Server Advisor is included with Pro. Manage subscription in Settings → Account.
advisor.welcomeBody: I can help with server setup, configs, mods, plugins, backups, and debugging. Describe your issue or goal, or paste an error from your server log.
advisor.mentionHint: Use @ in your message to mention a server (e.g. @My Server).
advisor.summaryDone: Updated {{files}} on {{serverName}}. Restart the server to apply changes.
advisor.summaryDoneNoServer: {{count}} action(s) completed. Restart the server if configs were changed.
advisor.emptyReplyFallback: Summary: Changes applied. Restart the server to apply. Need more tweaks?

home.subtitle: Create, manage, and share Minecraft servers with ease.
home.apiAiDesc: Create servers and run prompts via API. Talk to the Server Advisor for setup and config help. Manage keys on the website.
home.signInToSync: Sign in to sync backups and use Share.
home.firstTimeHint: Get started by creating your first Minecraft server!
home.viewLiveSyncHint: Servers → select server → Cloud to see files syncing.

onboarding.welcomeDesc: Your all-in-one Minecraft server manager. Create, configure, and share servers in seconds.
onboarding.navDesc: Switch between Home, Servers, and Settings using the navigation tabs at the top.
onboarding.createDesc: Click "Create Server" on the Home page or the + button in the server sidebar to set up a new Minecraft server.
onboarding.settingsDesc: Use the app menu (☰) or the Settings tab to personalize themes, language, and network settings.
onboarding.readyDesc: Explore, create, and enjoy hosting Minecraft servers. Have fun!

menu.aboutDescription: Create, manage, and share Minecraft servers.

closeConfirm.title: Close and stop servers?
closeConfirm.message: Closing will stop your running server. You have {{count}} server running.
closeConfirm.message_plural: Closing will stop all your running servers. You have {{count}} servers running.
closeConfirm.hint: With "Run in background" off, closing the window quits the app and stops any server. Turn it on in Settings to keep the app in the tray and servers running.

storage.title: Backups & storage
storage.signInPrompt: Sign in to view backups and storage
storage.noServersSynced: No servers synced yet. Go to Servers → select a server → Backup & Sync.
storage.noBackups: No backups yet. Create one from Servers → Backup & Sync.
storage.devPageHint: Open the dev menu for tools and devtools. Also available from the app menu (☰).

devPage.windowToolsDesc: Resize, presets, fit to screen, and debug log.

servers.title: Servers
servers.filesTabLabel: Local server files — browse and edit on this device
servers.selectOrCreate: Select a server or create one.
servers.manyServersBanner: You have many servers ({{count}}). Consider deleting one before creating a new one.
servers.deleteConfirmMessage: This will permanently delete "{{name}}" and all its files. This cannot be undone.
servers.startedInBackground: Server started in background. It will keep running when you close the app.
servers.relayConnected: Connected to relay. Give friends this address:
servers.relayRestartHint: If players get "Invalid session", restart the server once (online-mode is set for relay on each start).
servers.shareStartingHint: First time may download the tunnel tool (about a minute).
servers.shareTimeout: Tunnel is taking too long. Check your internet and try again.
servers.shareMethodUpnpDesc: Your IP, no relay. Works if router supports UPnP.
servers.firewallErrorUnknown: Could not add firewall rule. Try running the app as administrator.
servers.syncInSettings: Sync in Settings to show on website
servers.bigFilesUpgradeHint: Upgrade storage on the website to include them.
servers.fastSaveHint: Fast Save = snapshot + mini (basic server files). Check above to also upload big files.
servers.clickSyncToRegister: Click Sync to register this server
servers.clickStartToRegister: Start to register this server with the cloud and sync server info.
servers.syncFromSettings: Sync from Settings → Backup & Sync to show this server on the website.
servers.detailedReportOnWeb: Detailed file list and backup history are on the website.
servers.backupWebsiteMirror: The website mirrors your backups — same data here and on the web. Storage is combined for all your servers; track usage and manage storage tiers on the website.
servers.storageCombinedAllServers: Storage is combined for all your server data backed up.
servers.filesTooBigCount: {{count}} backup(s) had files too big to include.
servers.signInToSeeBackups: Sign in to see backups and storage usage.
servers.syncToSeeBackupsForServer: Sync this server in Settings → Backup & Sync to see its backups here.
servers.noBackupsForThisServer: No backups for this server yet. Upload from the website or after creating a backup.
servers.scanInDesktop: Scan server files in the desktop app to see the file tree and storage breakdown.
servers.scanServerHint: Scan to see which files go in small vs big storage and list of mods/plugins.
servers.scanLegend: small = mini synced · big = synced to cloud too
servers.fullBackupCtaHint: Fast Save uploads snapshot + mini (basic server files). Use Include big files to also upload large files, or create a full backup on the website.
servers.fullBackupDoneHint: This server has a full backup. View and download on the website.
servers.syncProgressLegend: Synced = uploaded this run. Unchanged = already on server (skipped). Failed = upload error.
servers.syncFailedHint: Failed = upload error (e.g. HTTP 500). Fix server/nginx limits and run Sync Files again.
servers.storageUsageTooltip: Storage used on cloud for all your servers
servers.iterationsHint: Save a snapshot on a schedule. Latest sync is always current; these add history.
servers.backupsEmptyHint: No snapshots yet. Turn on a schedule above or use Save now to create one.

management.noFeedbackYet: Run a command to see feedback here.
management.playersHint: Use the commands below to manage players on your server.
management.propertiesHint: Edit values and save. Most changes apply after a server restart.

dashboard.refreshHint: Click "Refresh players" to load the list.
dashboard.banPrompt: Player to ban (name or name, reason):

terminal.placeholder: Enter command (e.g. list, say Hello, stop)

settings.title: Settings
settings.accountDesc: Sign in in your browser, then paste the token here to connect.

backupSync.title: Backup & Sync
backupSync.autoBackupHint: Register all servers to the cloud when you open the app. New servers are also registered automatically when created. On by default.
backupSync.desc: Sync your server list to {{host}} so backups and status show on the website. Upload backups there or from here.
backupSync.appRole: This app is your sync agent. Set up here; the website only shows your report and backups.
backupSync.webRole: See backups, storage usage, and big-file report on {{host}}. No need to go there to sync — do that here.
backupSync.noBackend: Backend not configured. Set VITE_API_BASE_URL to sync and use backups (e.g. https://api.ihost.one).
backupSync.signIn: Sign in to sync your servers and use cloud backups.
backupSync.migrationCardSteps: The server database is missing the sync table. On the machine where the backend runs, open a terminal in the project root and run the command below (in the backend directory). Then restart the backend if it's already running.
backupSync.migrationCardHint: Using API: {{url}}. If you use ihost.one and already ran migrations there, pull the latest app code and tap Check again.
backupSync.noServers: No servers yet. Create a server to sync.
backupSync.serversCount: {{count}} server(s) on this device. Sync to show them on the website.
backupSync.syncNotAvailable: Sync not available. Run database migrations on the server (npm run db:migrate in backend).
backupSync.openWeb: Backups are stored privately on {{host}}. Open the website to upload, download, or see what's synced.
backupSync.storageTiersHint: Mini files (configs, mod lists) sync free. Big files need a storage tier — upgrade on the website when you need them.
backupSync.iterationsForNewServersHint: When on, new servers get 3h / daily / weekly snapshots by default. Turn off to disable for new servers only.
backupSync.iterationsForAllHint: Turn on 3h / daily / weekly snapshots for every server you have.
backupSync.iterationsEnabledForAll: Automatic backups enabled for all servers

settings.accountConnectHint: Sign in or create an account in your browser. Then paste the token back here to connect the app.
settings.accountConnectFooter: Use the link however you like (open in browser or copy). After signing in, paste the token here.
settings.accountConnectFooterAuto: The website opens in your browser. After signing in you'll return to the app automatically.
settings.returnToAppHint: After signing in on the website, choose "Use this account" and you'll be signed in here.
settings.openLinkMessage: You can open this link in your browser or copy it and use it however you like.
settings.profileDesc: Display name and username for your account.
settings.accountSettingsOnWeb: Profile, passkeys, and password are managed on the website. Open the link below to edit them.
settings.accountSettingsOnWebShort: Edit profile, passkeys, and password on the website when needed.
settings.tierFeatureModloader: Host any modloader (Vanilla, Paper, Forge, Fabric, etc.)
settings.tierFeatureBackup: Cloud backup (settings, mods list, server files)
settings.buyCreditsProDesc: Pro includes 500 free credits per month. Buy more packs below for extra capacity.
settings.buyCreditsFreeDesc: Use AI features by purchasing credit packs. Any plan can buy credits.
settings.buyCreditsHint: Credits are used for AI features in the app. Packs are one-time purchases.
settings.creditPacksComingSoon: Credit packs will be available soon. Check the website for updates.
settings.backendNotConfigured: Backend not configured. Set VITE_API_BASE_URL to enable account and billing.

dev.tabDesc: Stripe Live/Test and dev tier override. Only visible in dev builds; not shown to customers.
dev.intro: These options are for developers only. They do not appear in production builds.
dev.stripeModeDesc: Use Test to subscribe with card 4242… (no real charges). Toggle applies to Subscribe and Manage billing.
dev.switchTierDesc: Change your plan for testing. Requires dev tier secret from server (DEV_TIER_OVERRIDE_SECRET). Save it once to use the buttons without re-entering.
dev.enterSecret: Enter the dev tier secret (or save it first).
dev.overrideNotAllowed: Dev tier override is not enabled for this account on the server.
dev.signInRequired: Sign in to use developer options.
dev.settingsAsIcon: Settings as icon (right side of navbar)
dev.settingsAsIconDesc: When on, Settings appears as a gear icon on the right of the top bar instead of a tab. When off, Settings is a tab in the main nav.
dev.settingsAsIconOn: Settings shown as icon on the right.
dev.settingsAsIconOff: Settings shown as a tab in the main nav.
dev.simulateStorageFullDesc: Show storage as 98% full on Home so you can test UI when near limit. Display only; does not change real data.
dev.simulateStorageOn: Storage simulation on. Home shows ~98% full.
dev.unlimitedUsageDesc: Show usage as unlimited on Home for testing. Display only; API limits still apply.
dev.unlimitedUsageOn: Usage shown as unlimited on Home.
dev.unlimitedUsageOff: Usage display back to real values.

settings.billingNoUrl: Billing portal did not return a link.
settings.checkoutNoUrl: Checkout did not return a link.
settings.checkoutNoPriceHint: Billing isn't set up on the server. Ask your admin to add Stripe Price IDs or enable dev tier override so you can switch plans without paying (see docs/SERVER-BILLING-PROMPT.md).
settings.billingSetupHint: If Subscribe doesn't work, the server may need billing set up. Ask your admin to enable dev tier override or Stripe.
settings.websiteUrlNotSet: Website URL not set. Set VITE_WEBSITE_URL (or use default port 3020) for browser sign-in.
settings.waitingForSignIn: Waiting for you to sign in in the browser.
settings.signInAtWebsite: Sign in at {{url}} then choose "Use this account".
settings.couldNotCreateLinkHint: The app could not reach the server to create a unique session. Check that VITE_API_BASE_URL is set (e.g. https://api.ihost.one), your internet connection, and that the server is running.
settings.pasteTokenHint: After signing in, copy the token from the page and paste it below.
settings.pasteTokenErrorEmpty: Paste the token from the browser.
settings.pasteTokenErrorInvalid: Invalid or expired token. Sign in again in the browser and copy a new token.
settings.curseforgeKeySync: CurseForge API key (stored encrypted in your account)
settings.runInBackgroundDesc: When on, closing the window hides the app to the tray and servers keep running. When off, closing quits the app and stops any running server.
settings.aboutDesc: Cross-platform Minecraft server management with mod/plugin browsing, relay sharing, and an integrated bot controller.
settings.updatesHint: To check for updates, use Help (☰) → Check for updates in the title bar.
settings.networkTunnelDesc: To let friends connect from the internet without opening your router, use a tunnel service.
settings.relayDesc: Share server uses the iHost domain and relay servers to give friends a public address—no router or port forwarding needed. Test that your app can reach the relay.
settings.networkShareHint: Start your server, then use Share server in the Overview to get a public address.
settings.playitStep1: Create an account at playit.gg and open the account/setup page.
settings.playitStep2: Choose your app and get a Setup Code (it expires after a short time).
settings.playitStep3: Download and run the playit agent, then enter the Setup Code to link your account.
settings.playitStep4: In the agent, add a Minecraft Java tunnel and set the local address to localhost and your server port (e.g. 25565).
settings.ngrokStep1: Sign up at ngrok.com and open the dashboard to get your authtoken.
settings.ngrokStep2: Install the ngrok agent (Microsoft Store or download from ngrok.com).
settings.ngrokStep3: Add your authtoken: run « ngrok config add-authtoken YOUR_TOKEN » in a terminal.
settings.ngrokStep4: For Minecraft, run « ngrok tcp 25565 » (or your server port).
settings.cloudflareTunnelStep1: In Cloudflare Zero Trust, go to Network > Tunnels and create a tunnel.
settings.cloudflareTunnelStep2: Download and install cloudflared (Windows MSI or your OS).
settings.cloudflareTunnelStep3: Run as Administrator: cloudflared.exe service install YOUR_TOKEN.
settings.cloudflareTunnelStep4: In the tunnel config, add a TCP route to localhost and your server port (25565).
settings.firewallDesc: Allow inbound TCP on server ports so players can connect (LAN or after port forwarding). Each server uses its own port; add rules per server or for a single port below. Admin rights may be required.
settings.firewallPerServerHint: Add rules for all your servers’ ports:
settings.firewallAllPartial: Added rules for some ports; failed for: {{failed}}.
settings.firewallNoServers: No servers yet. Create a server first, or add a rule for a single port below.
settings.firewallErrorFallback: Could not add firewall rule. Try running the app as administrator.
settings.upnpDesc: UPnP lets your router open a port automatically when you use Share server. Test whether your router supports UPnP (a short-lived mapping is created then removed).
settings.upnpError: UPnP test failed (router may not support it or it may be disabled).
settings.upnpErrorRouterNotFound: Router didn't respond. UPnP may be disabled on your router or it may be unreachable. Use Share server for a tunnel instead.

wizard.name: Name
wizard.portHint: Leave empty for next free port (25565, 25566, …)
wizard.noBundledJava: No bundled Java. You can download it after creating the server.
wizard.creatingMinimizedHint: “{{name}}” is being created in the background. Click the server in the sidebar to view progress.
wizard.manyServersConfirm: You have many servers ({{count}}). Consider deleting one before creating a new one. Create anyway?

files.selectServer: Select a server to browse and edit files.

mods.selectServer: Select a server to browse mods and plugins.
mods.searchHint: Search for mods or plugins to install.
mods.noResults: No results. Try a different search.

import.title: Import from server
import.description: Enter a server address to read its version. A new local server will be created with the same Minecraft version.

error.readFailed: Read failed. The server may have closed the connection early (e.g. proxy or firewall).

tools.title: Window Tools
tools.danceDesc: Watch the window dance through sizes — a quick show-off of how fast the app responds.
